No Poverty.reason: The course explicitly critiques economic growth for creating 'severe wealth inequality' and 'exploitation,' directly aligning with SDG 1's focus on reducing poverty and promoting pro-poor strategies.
evidence: 創造更嚴重的貧富不均 (creating more severe wealth inequality)
普羅大眾持續遭受的剝削 (the general public continues to suffer from exploitation)

Zero Hunger.reason: The course explicitly addresses 'food' and 'farming' as areas for redesign and includes a lecture 'Where does your food come from?', directly supporting sustainable food systems (Target 2.4) and food security.
evidence: 你的食物從哪來？ (Where does your food come from?)

Good Health and Well-being.reason: The course directly discusses 'depriving us of our happiness,' 'globalization and mental health,' and 'eco-anxiety,' strongly aligning with promoting mental health and well-being (Target 3.4).
evidence: 剝奪我們的幸福 (depriving us of our happiness)
全球化與心理健康 (globalization and mental health)

Quality Education.reason: As a general education course on 'degrowth' and alternative economic systems, it inherently serves as education for sustainable development, inspiring students to contribute to a 'global movement' for well-being (Target 4.7).
evidence: 激發出同學們這些潛能 (inspire students' potential)
為此全球運動提供重要貢獻 (contribute to this global movement)
以人民幸福快樂為目的的社會與經濟制度 (a socio-economic system aimed at people's happiness)

Gender Equality.reason: While the course critiques 'exploitation' and 'inequality,' there is no explicit mention or strong inference related to gender-specific issues or targets within the syllabus.
evidence: 普羅大眾持續遭受的剝削 (the general public continues to suffer from exploitation)

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence related to clean water, sanitation, or hygiene was found in the course syllabus.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: The course includes 'energy' as an area for redesign and features a lecture questioning 'green growth' and discussing 'green energy development,' directly engaging with clean energy concepts (Target 7.2).
evidence: 綠色成長存在嗎？ (Does green growth exist?)
綠能發展的第一現場 (The front line of green energy development)

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course's central theme is 'Degrowth,' a direct critique of 'endless economic growth' and GDP, proposing alternatives like 'cooperatives' for well-being, fundamentally challenging and redefining economic growth (Target 8.1, 8.4).
evidence: 拋開經濟成長枷鎖 (cast off the shackles of economic growth)

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course proposes redesigning 'housing' and 'transportation' and discusses 'cooperatives' as alternative structures, linking to sustainable infrastructure development (Target 9.1) and innovative economic models.
evidence: 合作社：原來公司可以長這樣？ (Cooperatives: So a company can be like this?)

Reduced Inequalities.reason: A core premise is that economic growth creates 'more severe wealth inequality,' 'exploitation,' and 'worsening distribution,' directly addressing the reduction of inequalities and promoting inclusion (Targets 10.2, 10.4).
evidence: 創造更嚴重的貧富不均 (creating more severe wealth inequality)
普羅大眾持續遭受的剝削 (the general public continues to suffer from exploitation)

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: The course explicitly covers 'housing justice,' 'housing cooperatives,' and 'ecovillages,' directly supporting adequate housing (Target 11.1) and sustainable human settlement planning (Target 11.3).

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: The course critiques 'consumerism' and features 'zero waste' and 'repair cafes,' directly promoting sustainable consumption and production patterns, waste reduction (Target 12.5), and sustainable lifestyles (Target 12.8).

Climate Action.reason: The course's foundational premise is that 'endless economic growth is destroying the planet's ecology,' explicitly mentioning 'global warming' and 'eco-anxiety,' directly motivating climate action and awareness (Targets 13.2, 13.3).
evidence: 無止境的經濟成長正在毀滅地球生態 (endless economic growth is destroying the planet's ecology)

Life Below Water.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence related to marine ecosystems, ocean health, or life below water was found in the course syllabus.

Life on Land.reason: The course emphasizes that growth 'is destroying the planet's ecology' and includes a lecture on 'biodiversity,' directly addressing the conservation of terrestrial ecosystems and halting biodiversity loss (Target 15.5).
evidence: 毀滅地球生態 (destroying the planet's ecology)
「生物多樣性」？干我Ｘ事 ('Biodiversity'? What the X does it have to do with me)

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course promotes 'housing justice' and 'political democracy and economic democracy' as alternatives to exploitative systems, supporting inclusive decision-making and access to justice (Targets 16.3, 16.7).
evidence: 政治民主與經濟民主 (political democracy and economic democracy)

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course discusses contributing to a 'global movement' and 'cross-disciplinary social movement linkage,' reflecting multi-stakeholder partnerships (Target 17.16), and critiques GDP, aligning with alternative progress measurements (Target 17.19).
evidence: 福祉經濟跨領域社運串聯 (cross-disciplinary social movement linkage for a well-being economy)
衡量經濟成長的工具—GDP—盲目堆疊 (The tool for measuring economic growth—GDP—blindly piles up)
